items.scenario: You recently signed up for a weekend kayaking excursion organized by a local outdoor club. The confirmation email lists a meeting point far from the address advertised on the club's website, and you are unsure which location is correct.
items.direction: Write an email to the excursion organizer. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain which details in the confirmation seem to contradict each other
Ask which meeting location is the correct one
Suggest a way to confirm the final plan before Saturday
items.subject: Meeting point for Saturday's kayaking excursion

items.scenario: You are a second-year biology major hoping to enroll in an advanced genetics seminar that is already full. The course requires the instructor's permission, and your academic advisor believes your background makes you a strong candidate for one of the last open spots.
items.direction: Write an email to the seminar instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe your relevant coursework and background
Explain why the seminar fits your academic goals
Ask what steps are needed to secure a spot
items.subject: Permission to enroll in the genetics seminar

items.scenario: You ordered a pair of noise-canceling headphones from an online retailer three weeks ago. They finally arrived, but the left earcup produces a constant crackling sound, and the return instructions on the website lead to a page that will not load.
items.direction: Write an email to the retailer's support team. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the defect you noticed after unboxing the headphones
Mention the error you hit on the return page
Ask how to obtain a working replacement
items.subject: Faulty headphones and an inaccessible return page

items.scenario: You recently began a part-time internship at an architecture firm. Your supervisor mentioned that interns are sometimes allowed to shadow the site-visit team, and you would like to take part, but you are unsure how to raise the request appropriately.
items.direction: Write an email to your internship supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain your interest in joining the site visits
Describe the skills you hope to build there
Ask how you might join an upcoming visit
items.subject: Joining the site-visit team as an intern
